goal: TH daily poster reading text rendered ragged/uncentered
root_cause: wrapTextLines joined tokens with a space (`${line} ${token}`), and tokenizeText, for a no-space Thai sentence wider than the line, fell back to Array.from(value) = per-character tokens. Result: long Thai readings rendered as SPACE-SEPARATED glyphs wrapped raggedly — looked uncentered/broken vs clean English.
fix: tokenizeText now returns {tokens, sep}: sep=' ' for word-split (English unchanged), sep='' for the no-space char-split path (Thai/CJK). wrapTextLines joins with that sep, so Thai wraps into clean lines with no inserted spaces; each line still drawn textAlign:center at width/2 → properly centered. Touches only the text-wrapping helpers, not the asset/payload pipeline.
verified: node --check ok; mock-ctx logic test: long no-space Thai → 2 clean lines, zero spaces inserted; English → still space-joined multi-word lines. md5 parity canonical=www=ios. (Full canvas/font render not re-run — text-wrap logic is the fix; recommend an eyeball on a real TH daily poster.)
notes: Improves Thai wrapping on ALL posters that use wrapTextLines (daily/question/celtic), not just daily.
